verdict: bare-ratio route FAILS (chi3/chi2=9.05>5.23, r2_bare=0.866>0.5); Brazovskii sea strong-coupling; closure requires the COMMON-MODE-SUBTRACTED susceptibilities (the genuine residual)

claims.name: bare_ratio_exceeds_threshold
claims.detail: (bare chi^(3)/chi^(2) = 9.05 > 1/(2a0) = 5.23: the operator's literal bare-ratio bound FAILS; r_2(bare) = a0*9.05 = 0.866 > 1/2 -- geometric domination breaks at the base order if bare susceptibilities are used)

claims.name: bare_ratios_strong_coupling
claims.detail: (int G^(n+1)/int G^n -> 2.513 ~ 1/rhat = 2.970: the bare cumulant ratios are O(1/rhat) strong-coupling, growing -- the Brazovskii sea is strongly fluctuating)

claims.name: scscope_is_subtracted_not_bare
claims.detail: (SC-SCOPE n=3 difference ~0.04 << r_2(bare)=0.866: the measured small value is the COMMON-MODE-SUBTRACTED (pattern-dependent) ratio, not the bare chi^(3) -- confirming the closure must use the subtracted susceptibilities, and the bare common part cancels in the difference)

claims.name: residual_is_subtracted_bound
claims.detail: (HONEST NEGATIVE on the bare route: the genuine RES-5 residual is to bound the COMMON-MODE-SUBTRACTED ratio chi_pd^(k+1)/chi_pd^(k) (after all-order subtraction), a strong-coupling computation -- NOT the elementary bare-ratio bound, which fails)
